title: iberis linifolia subsp. boppardensis
intro: Iberis linifolia subsp. boppardensis, also known as Boppard's candytuft, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb with a long history of medicinal use, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its expectorant, carminative, and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Iberis linifolia subsp. boppardensis can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
